metadata.title: Claude, Pragmatic.
metadata.description: Skill teardown: hai — the Humanitarians AI Pragmatist cut. Method-first register, when NOT to use and where it fails, CLI worked exercise, Irreducibly-Human tangent. Self-demo: Phase 2 register rewrite.

beats.narration_text: Bonjour, Liam. Liam, in for Bear. Today: hai — the Humanitarians AI Pragmatist cut. You give it a reel or a lecture, it forks the beat sheet, rewrites every narration in the Pragmatist register — method first, when to use it, and crucially when NOT to and where it fails — adds a CLI worked exercise beat, and closes with the Humanitarians AI outro.

props.runningText: * scaffold → pragmatist rewrite → CLI exercise → outro...

beats.narration_text: The hai skill has one register: Pragmatist. The audience is HAI Fellows — busy practitioners who need to act, not theorize. The anatomy: the hai brand data file holds register rules, voice config, humanitarians palette, outro spec. brand_variant.py scaffolds the hai- directory and marks every narration as a todo. The Pragmatist voice guide specifies the method-first structure: lead with what it is and what it produces, state when to use it, then the required when-not-to. Voice is Kokoro am_onyx — free by default. ElevenLabs override is available but not the default.

props.title: Pragmatist register. Busy practitioners. Method first.
props.calloutSub: Humanitarians palette: cream / ink / teal / crimson

beats.narration_text: Six phases. One: scaffold — brand_variant.py creates the hai- directory, marks all narrations as todos. Two: rewrite in Pragmatist register — method, when to use, when NOT to, no personality tax, no hedging. Three: optional Irreducibly-Human tangent — one bounded aside per video, only if a clean opportunity appears, never forced. Four: insert the CLI worked exercise beat — a paste-ready claude command the viewer runs today. Five: HAI outro. Six: build — GATE P first, then Kokoro audio.

props.title: 6 phases: scaffold → rewrite → tangent → CLI → outro → build

beats.narration_text: Before the mechanism, let me show you Phase 2 — the Pragmatist register rewrite. I am going to take one canonical narration and rewrite it for HAI Fellows. The canonical version explains. The Pragmatist version leads with method, states when to use it, and adds the required when NOT to.

props.command: Rewrite one beat in Pragmatist register
output: Pragmatist: method / when to use / when NOT to

beats.narration_text: Here is the canonical narration: 'The shot-planner skill reads a beat sheet and assigns each beat a render medium — Manim, Remotion, image generation, or video generation — based on what maximizes learning for that beat type.' Now the Pragmatist rewrite for HAI Fellows: Method: shot-planner reads a beat sheet and assigns render medium per beat. When to use it: you have a beat sheet with open shot fields and want a reasoned medium assignment before any rendering spend. When NOT to: skip it if the beat sheet already has media assigned, or if you are building a terminal-screencast — the medium is fixed. Where it fails: it cannot predict whether a Manim scene will actually render correctly; always verify the render before spending on audio.

props.title: Phase 2 — Pragmatist register rewrite
props.code:
SOURCE (claude-liam):
"The shot-planner skill reads a beat sheet and assigns
each beat a render medium — Manim, Remotion, image
generation, or video generation — based on what
maximizes learning for that beat type."

PRAGMATIST REWRITE (HAI Fellows):
Method: shot-planner reads a beat sheet and assigns
render medium per beat.
When to use: beat sheet has open shot fields;
you want a reasoned medium assignment before spend.
When NOT to: skip if media already assigned, or if
building a terminal-screencast (medium is fixed).
Where it fails: cannot predict whether a Manim scene
will render correctly — always verify before audio spend.

beats.narration_text: The first mechanism: when NOT to and where it fails. This is not a caveat tacked onto the end. The SKILL.md calls it the AI main event for busy practitioners. A HAI Fellow reads to act. If the conditions are wrong, they need to know before they start, not after. Every Pragmatist beat must include the failure mode and the non-use case. If you cannot identify them, the narration is not ready.

props.heading: When NOT to. Where it fails. Required.
props.body: The failure mode is not a caveat — it is the AI main event for busy practitioners. A HAI Fellow reads to act. If the conditions are wrong, they need to know before starting. Every Pragmatist beat must include the non-use case and the failure mode. If you cannot identify them, the narration is not ready.
props.quote: Required: when NOT to and where it fails — the AI main event for busy practitioners.

beats.narration_text: The second mechanism: the Irreducibly-Human tangent. Most reels get none. If and only if a clean opportunity appears, add one bounded aside — one per video. The SKILL.md quotes it exactly: 'This the AI does well. This is the human's — it cannot be handed off.' A decision boundary, not a sermon. The re-entry cue is mandatory. If you find yourself forcing it, that is a sign the reel does not have a natural IH boundary — skip it.

props.heading: Irreducibly Human. One per video. Never forced.
props.body: Most reels get no IH tangent. One bounded aside only when a clean opportunity exists — a decision boundary, not a sermon. The re-entry cue is mandatory. If you are forcing it, skip it. The tangent must name what the AI cannot substitute, not just what humans prefer.
props.quote: This the AI does well. This is the human's — it cannot be handed off.

beats.narration_text: The third mechanism: the slate is a starting point, not a locked master. The SKILL.md is explicit: HAI Fellows take the variant and refine it further with Claude Code. The hai cut is strong on doctrine, but it is not the finished product. The practitioner applies it to their specific context, their data, their decision. The variant optimizes for the general case. The Fellow optimizes for the actual case. That gap is where the value is.

props.heading: Strong on doctrine. Refined on context.
props.body: The hai variant optimizes for the general case. The HAI Fellow optimizes for the actual case — their data, their decision, their domain. The gap between the two is where the value is. The slate is not the finished product; it is the strong on-doctrine starting point that the Fellow takes further.
props.quote: HAI Fellows take the slate cut and refine it further with Claude Code — the variant is a strong on-doctrine starting point, not a locked master.

beats.narration_text: Claude, Pragmatic. The hai skill delivers four things: an hai- directory with the canonical untouched, every narration rewritten for HAI Fellows in the Pragmatist register — method, when to use, when NOT to, where it fails — a CLI worked exercise beat the viewer runs today, and the Humanitarians AI outro. The Irreducibly-Human tangent is optional, one per video, never forced.

artifactLines: hai- directory — canonical beat_sheet.json never touched
Pragmatist rewrite: method / when-to-use / when NOT to / where it fails
CLI worked exercise beat — paste-ready claude command
IH tangent (0–1 per video) + Humanitarians AI outro

beats.narration_text: Two questions before you run hai. First: have you read the Pragmatist voice guide — voices/pragmatist/VOICE.md — so your rewrites lead with method and include the required failure mode? Second: is the canonical beat_sheet.json untouched? The scaffold creates the hai- directory; the source is never the rewrite target.
